provides shared session closing functionality with summary generation.\n// This allows both CommandHandler and ServiceHandler to reuse the same closing logic.\ntype SessionCloser struct {\n\tdb       *DB\n\tsender   *Sender\n\tproxyURL string\n\tclient   *http.Client\n\tptyMgr   *PTYManager\n}\n\n// NewSessionCloser creates a SessionCloser with the given dependencies.\nfunc NewSessionCloser(db as closed.\n// This is the shared implementation used by both /close command and service messages.\nfunc (sc *SessionCloser) CloseSessionWithSummary(ctx context.Context, chatID, threadID int64, group *Group) error {\n\tsession, err := sc.db.GetSession(ctx, chatID, threadID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"look up session: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tif session == nil {\n\t\treturn closing\n\tsummary, summaryErr := sc.generateSessionSummary(ctx, session, group)\n\tif summaryErr != nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[bridge/closer] generate summary failed for (%d, %d): %v\", chatID, threadID, summaryErr)\n\t\t// Continue with closing even if summary fails\n\t}\n\n\tif summary != \"\" {\n\t\t// Send the summary as a new message in the topic\n\t\ttidPtr := &threadID\n\t\tsummaryText := fmt.Sprintf(\"📋 <b>Session Summary</b>\\n\\n%s\", summary)\n\t\tsendReq := contract.SendRequest{\n\t\t\tChatID:   chatID,\n\t\t\tThreadID: tidPtr,\n\t\t\tText: threadID, storeErr)\n\t\t\t// Non-fatal: continue anyway\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif err := sc.db.CloseSession(ctx, chatID, threadID); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"close session: %w\", via a transient PTY pane.\nfunc (sc *SessionCloser) generateSessionSummary(ctx context.Context, session *Session, group *Group) (string, error) {\n\tsummCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 120*time.Second)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\tpaneName := fmt.Sprintf(\"sum-%d\", time.Now().UnixNano())\n\tpermArgs := resolvePermissionArgs(group)\n\targs := append(permArgs,\n\t\t\"--model\", \"claude-haiku-4-5\",\n\t)\n\tif session.SessionID != \"\" {\n\t\targs = append(args, \"--resume\", session.SessionID)\n\t}\n\n\tpaneTarget, err := sc.ptyMgr.SpawnPane(paneName, group.CWD, args)\n\tif It processes service messages and\n// generates summaries when topics are closed via the Telegram UI.\nfunc (h *ServiceHandler) Handle(ctx context.Context, update contract.Update) {\n\tif update.Service == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// We only care about forum_topic_closed events\n\tif update.Service.Type != contract.ServiceTypeForumTopicClosed {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Need thread_id to know which topic was closed\n\tif update.ThreadID == nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[bridge/service] forum_topic_closed without thread_id, chat %d\", update.ChatID)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the group for this chat\n\tgroup, err := h.db.GetGroup(ctx, update.ChatID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[bridge/service] get group failed for chat %d: %v\", update.ChatID, err)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif group == nil {\n\t\t// Group not registered - ignore\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate summary and close the session\n\tif err := h.sessionCloser.CloseSessionWithSummary(ctx, update.ChatID, *update.ThreadID, group); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[bridge/service] close session with summary failed for (%d, %d): %v\", update.ChatID, *update.ThreadID, err)\n\t}\n}\n","numLines":243,"startLine":1,"totalLines":243}}}

/file/{file_id}.\n379\t// It calls Telegram's getFile to resolve the file_path, then streams the bytes from\n380\t// the Telegram file CDN. Returns 404 for invalid/expired file_id, 413 if > 20MB.\n381\tfunc handleFile(s *telegram.Sender) http.HandlerFunc {\n382\t\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n383\t\t\tfileID := r.PathValue(\"file_id\")\n384\t\t\tif fileID == \"\" {\n385\t\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, 400, \"missing file_id\")\n386\t\t\t\treturn\n387\t\t\t}\n388\t\n389\t\t\ttgFile, apiErr := s.GetFile(r.Context(), fileID)\n390\t\t\tif apiErr != nil {\n391\t\t\t\t// Telegram 400-range errors mean invalid or expired file_id.\n392\t\t\t\tif apiErr.ErrorCode >= 400 && apiErr.ErrorCode < 500 {\n393\t\t\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusNotFound, 404, \"file not found or expired\")\n394\t\t\t\t\treturn\n395\t\t\t\t}\n396\t\t\t\twriteTelegramError(w, apiErr)\n397\t\t\t\treturn\n398\t\t\t}\n399\t\n400\t\t\tif tgFile.FilePath == nil {\n401\t\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusNotFound, 404, \"file not available\")\n402\t\t\t\treturn\n403\t\t\t}\n404\t\n405\t\t\tif tgFile.FileSize != nil && *tgFile.FileSize > telegram.MaxFileSize {\n406\t\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusRequestEntityTooLarge, 413, \"file exceeds 20MB limit\")\n407\t\t\t\treturn\n408\t\t\t}\n409\t\n410\t\t\tresp, err := s.DownloadFile(r.Context(), *tgFile.FilePath)\n411\t\t\tif err != nil {\n412\t\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusBadGateway, 502, fmt.Sprintf(\"download failed: %v\", err))\n413\t\t\t\treturn\n414\t\t\t}\n415\t\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n416\t\n417\t\t\tif resp.StatusCode == http.StatusNotFound {\n418\t\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusNotFound, 404, \"file not found or expired\")\n419\t\t\t\treturn\n420\t\t\t}\n421\t\t\tif resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {\n422\t\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusBadGateway, 502, fmt.Sprintf(\"Telegram returned %d\", resp.StatusCode))\n423\t\t\t\treturn\n424\t\t\t}\n425\t\n426\t\t\tct := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n427\t\t\tif ct == \"\" {\n428\t\t\t\tct = \"application/octet-stream\"\n429\t\t\t}\n430\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", ct)\n431\t\n432\t\t\tif cl := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Length\"); cl != \"\" {\n433\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", cl)\n434\t\t\t}\n435\t\n436\t\t\tfilename := path.Base(*tgFile.FilePath)\n437\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Disposition\", fmt.Sprintf(`attachment; filename=%q`, filename))\n438\t\n439\t\t\tif _, err := io.Copy(w, resp.Body); err != nil {\n440\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"file stream %s: %v\", fileID, err)\n441\t\t\t}\n442\t\t}\n443\t}\n444\t\n445\tconst (\n446\t\tmaxPhotoSize    = 10 * 1024 * 1024 // 10MB\n447\t\tmaxMediaSize    = 50 * 1024 * 1024 // 50MB for document/audio/video\n448\t\tmultipartMemory = 32 * 1024 * 1024 // max memory for multipart parsing (rest goes to disk)\n449\t)\n450\t\n451\t// parseMediaForm parses a multipart form with the given size limit and returns the\n452\t// required chat_id. Returns false and writes an error if parsing or chat_id fails.\n453\tfunc parseMediaForm(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, maxSize int64) (chatID int64, ok bool) {\n454\t\tr.Body = http.MaxBytesReader(w, r.Body, maxSize+4096) // +4096 for form fields\n455\t\tif err := r.ParseMultipartForm(multipartMemory); err != nil {\n456\t\t\tvar maxErr *http.MaxBytesError\n457\t\t\tif errors.As(err, &maxErr) {\n458\t\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusRequestEntityTooLarge, 413, fmt.Sprintf(\"file exceeds %dMB limit\", maxSize/1024/1024))\n459\t\t\t} else {\n460\t\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, 400, \"invalid multipart form: \"+err.Error())\n461\t\t\t}\n462\t\t\treturn 0, false\n463\t\t}\n464\t\tchatIDStr := r.FormValue(\"chat_id\")\n465\t\tchatID, err := strconv.ParseInt(chatIDStr, 10, 64)\n466\t\tif err != nil || chatID == 0 {\n467\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, 400, \"invalid or missing chat_id\")\n468\t\t\treturn 0, false\n469\t\t}\n470\t\treturn chatID, true\n471\t}\n472\t\n473\t// optionalInt64 parses a form value as int64, returning nil if absent.\n474\tfunc optionalInt64(r *http.Request, key string) *int64 {\n475\t\tif v := r.FormValue(key); Returns 404 for invalid/expired file_id, 413 if > 20MB.\nfunc handleFile(s *telegram.Sender) http.HandlerFunc {\n\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tfileID := r.PathValue(\"file_id\")\n\t\tif fileID == \"\" {\n\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, 400, \"missing file_id\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\ttgFile, apiErr := s.GetFile(r.Context(), fileID)\n\t\tif apiErr != nil {\n\t\t\t// Telegram 400-range errors mean invalid or expired file_id.\n\t\t\tif apiErr.ErrorCode >= 400 && apiErr.ErrorCode < 500 {\n\t\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusNotFound, 404, \"file not found or expired\")\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\twriteTelegramError(w, apiErr)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif tgFile.FilePath == nil {\n\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusNotFound, 404, \"file not available\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif tgFile.FileSize != nil && *tgFile.FileSize > telegram.MaxFileSize {\n\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusRequestEntityTooLarge, 413, \"file exceeds 20MB limit\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresp, err := s.DownloadFile(r.Context(), *tgFile.FilePath)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusBadGateway, 502, fmt.Sprintf(\"download failed: %v\", err))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n\n\t\tif resp.StatusCode == http.StatusNotFound {\n\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusNotFound, 404, \"file not found or expired\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tif resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {\n\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusBadGateway, 502, fmt.Sprintf(\"Telegram returned %d\", resp.StatusCode))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tct := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n\t\tif ct == \"\" {\n\t\t\tct = \"application/octet-stream\"\n\t\t}\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", ct)\n\n\t\tif cl := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Length\"); cl != \"\" {\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", cl)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfilename := path.Base(*tgFile.FilePath)\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Disposition\", fmt.Sprintf(`attachment; filename=%q`, filename))\n\n\t\tif _, err := io.Copy(w, resp.Body); err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"file stream %s: %v\", fileID, err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nconst (\n\tmaxPhotoSize    = 10 * 1024 * 1024 // 10MB\n\tmaxMediaSize    = 50 * 1024 * 1024 // 50MB for document/audio/video\n\tmultipartMemory = 32 * 1024 * 1024 // max memory for multipart parsing (rest goes to disk)\n)\n\n// parseMediaForm parses a multipart form with the given size limit and returns the\n// required chat_id. Returns false and writes an error if parsing or chat_id fails.\nfunc parseMediaForm(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, maxSize int64) (chatID int64, ok bool) {\n\tr.Body = http.MaxBytesReader(w, r.Body, maxSize+4096) // +4096 for form fields\n\tif err := r.ParseMultipartForm(multipartMemory); err != nil {\n\t\tvar maxErr *http.MaxBytesError\n\t\tif errors.As(err, &maxErr) {\n\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusRequestEntityTooLarge, 413, fmt.Sprintf(\"file exceeds %dMB limit\", maxSize/1024/1024))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, 400, \"invalid multipart form: \"+err.Error())\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn 0, false\n\t}\n\tchatIDStr := r.FormValue(\"chat_id\")\n\tchatID, err := strconv.ParseInt(chatIDStr, 10, 64)\n\tif err != nil || chatID == 0 {\n\t\twriteProxyError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, 400, \"invalid or missing chat_id\")\n\t\treturn 0, false\n\t}\n\treturn chatID, true\n}\n\n// optionalInt64 parses a form value as int64, returning nil if absent.\nfunc optionalInt64(r *http.Request, key string) *int64 {\n\tif v := r.FormValue(key); v != \"\" {\n\t\tif n, err := strconv.ParseInt(v, 10, 64); err == nil {\n\t\t\treturn &n\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// optionalInt parses a form value as int, returning nil if absent.\nfunc optionalInt(r *http.Request, key string) *int {\n\tif v := r.FormValue(key); {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_5bf8e49545544b769e5086a4","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# telegram-claude-bridge\n2\t\n3\tA bridge that connects a Telegram bot to [Claude Code](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code) CLI sessions, letting users interact with Claude directly from Telegram group forum topics. Each topic gets a persistent Claude Code session running in tmux; the bridge routes messages in, streams responses back, and keeps sessions warm between turns. Uses Anthropic subscription billing (interactive PTY), not API credits.\n4\t\n5\t---\n6\t\n7\t## How it works\n8\t\n9\tThe system is split into two processes:\n10\t\n11\t**Proxy** — A lightweight container that holds the Telegram bot token, long-polls the Telegram `getUpdates` endpoint, and exposes an internal HTTP API (`/updates`, `/send`, `/edit`, etc.) for the bridge to consume. It holds no session state.\n12\t\n13\t**Bridge** — The stateful brain, running as a systemd service on the bare-metal host. It polls the proxy, manages Claude Code sessions, spawns `claude` inside tmux panes (one pane per forum topic), streams responses back via the proxy, and stores session metadata in a local SQLite database.\n14\t\n15\t**Dashboard** (optional) — A Bubbletea TUI that displays real-time session health, in-flight messages, and cost data over a Unix socket.\n16\t\n17\t### Claude invocation\n18\t\n19\tThe bridge maintains a tmux session named `telegram-bridge`. Each active Telegram forum topic maps to one tmux window. Claude Code is launched with configurable permissions:\n20\t\n21\t```\n22\tclaude <permission_flags> --model <model> [--resume <session_id>]\n23\t```\n24\t\n25\tPermission flags are determined by the group's `permission_mode` setting:\n26\t- `bypassPermissions` → `--dangerously-skip-permissions`\n27\t- `acceptEdits`, `plan`, `dontAsk` → `--permission-mode <mode>`\n28\t\n29\t**Note:** The current implementation always passes `--dangerously-skip-permissions` regardless of the stored `permission_mode` (tracked issue). The `permission_mode` is configurable via `/config permission_mode <mode>` or `/permission <mode>`, but does not yet affect runtime behavior.\n30\t\n31\tPanes stay warm between messages (45 s idle threshold). A Claude stop-hook writes the final response to a file that the bridge polls; it falls back to PTY screen-scraping if the hook is not configured. On session ID loss, up to 40 messages of history are prepended from SQLite to restore context.\n32\t\n33\t---\n34\t\n35\t## Features\n36\t\n37\t### Notification modes\n38\t\n39\tPer-topic, configurable via `/notify` or natural language:\n40\t\n41\t| Mode | Behavior |\n42\t|------|----------|\n43\t| `live` (default) | Progressively edits one Telegram message as Claude streams (1 s debounce) |\n44\t| `summary` | Posts a placeholder, edits to the final response only when done |\n45\t| `quiet` | No updates during processing; posts only on completion |\n46\t\n47\t### Natural language intent detection\n48\t\n49\tThe bridge intercepts common phrases before forwarding them to Claude:\n50\t\n51\t- **Cancel:** \"cancel\", \"stop\", \"abort\"\n52\t- **Model switch:** \"use opus\", \"think harder\", \"fast mode\", etc.\n53\t- **Notify mode:** \"stream updates\", \"quiet mode\", etc.\n54\t- **Cost / status queries**\n55\t- **Session control:** \"close session\", \"new session\"\n56\t- **Timeout adjustments**\n57\t\n58\t### Media support\n59\t\n60\t| Type | Handling |\n61\t|------|----------|\n62\t| Photos | Injected as image attachments (10 MB limit) |\n63\t| Voice messages | Transcribed via `whisper` CLI, text prepended to prompt |\n64\t| Audio files (MP3, M4A, FLAC, WAV, OGG) | Same as voice messages |\n65\t| Video / video notes | Keyframes extracted + audio transcribed; both fed to Claude |\n66\t| Documents | Passed as file attachments (50 MB limit) |\n67\t\n68\t### Dispatcher / orchestrator mode\n69\t\n70\tWhen enabled (`/dispatch on`), Claude receives two synthetic tools:\n71\t\n72\t- `spawn_worker` — the bridge spawns a headless Claude instance and injects the result back\n73\t- `update_progress` — posts a status message to the topic immediately\n74\t\n75\t### Self-update\n76\t\n77\tThe bridge checks a configurable remote for new versions on a background interval and applies updates with `/update do`. Update checking can be disabled by setting `UPDATE_INTERVAL_MINUTES=0`.\n78\t\n79\t### Session lifecycle\n80\t\n81\t- One session per `(chat_id, thread_id)` pair\n82\t- Messages arriving during processing are batched in a per-topic queue (32 messages deep)\n83\t- Sessions resume across restarts via `--resume <session_id>`\n84\t- Stale sessions cleaned up automatically (configurable interval and TTL)\n85\t- On close: Claude (Haiku) generates a summary and pins it to the topic\n86\t\n87\t---\n88\t\n89\t## Prerequisites\n90\t\n91\tThe following must be available on the host running the bridge:\n92\t\n93\t- **tmux** — session multiplexer that hosts Claude Code panes\n94\t- **claude** — [Claude Code CLI](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code), logged in with an active Anthropic subscription\n95\t- **whisper** — [whisper.cpp](https://github.com/ggerganov/whisper.cpp) or compatible CLI (required only for voice/audio transcription)\n96\t- **git** — required only if self-update is enabled\n97\t\n98\tThe proxy runs as a Docker container and has no host-level dependencies beyond a container runtime.\n99\t\n100\t---\n101\t\n102\t## Configuration\n103\t\n104\t### Proxy environment variables\n105\t\n106\t| Variable | Default | Description |\n107\t|----------|---------|-------------|\n108\t| `BOT_TOKEN` (or `TELEGRAM_TOKEN`) | — | **Required.** Telegram bot token. |\n109\t| `PROXY_LISTEN_ADDR` | `:8080` | HTTP listen address |\n110\t| `PROXY_DB_PATH` | `proxy.db` | SQLite DB path for offset tracking |\n111\t| `POLL_TIMEOUT` | `30` | Telegram long-poll timeout (seconds) |\n112\t\n113\t### Bridge environment variables\n114\t\n115\t| Variable | Default | Description |\n116\t|----------|---------|-------------|\n117\t| `PROXY_URL` | — | **Required.** Base URL of the proxy (e.g. `http://localhost:8080`) |\n118\t| `BRIDGE_DB_PATH` | `bridge.db` | SQLite DB path |\n119\t| `ALLOWED_CHAT_ID` | `0` (all) | Restrict to a single Telegram chat ID |\n120\t| `POLL_TIMEOUT` | `30` | Long-poll timeout (seconds) |\n121\t| `SESSION_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MINUTES` | `60` | Stale session cleanup interval (`0` = disabled) |\n122\t| `SESSION_TTL_HOURS` | `168` (7 days) | Age at which a session is considered stale |\n123\t| `CLOSE_INACTIVE_TOPICS` | `false` | Close Telegram topics for stale sessions |\n124\t| `ADMIN_USER_ID` | `0` | Bootstrap initial admin on startup |\n125\t| `ADMIN_CHAT_ID` | `0` | Chat ID for crash-loop alerts (via ExecStopPost) |\n126\t| `EVENT_PUBLISHING_ENABLED` | `false` | Enable dashboard Unix socket events |\n127\t| `EVENT_SOCKET_PATH` | `/tmp/telegram-bridge-events.sock` | Dashboard socket path |\n128\t| `UPDATE_INTERVAL_MINUTES` | `5` | Self-update check interval (`0` = disabled) |\n129\t\n130\t---\n131\t\n132\t## Commands reference\n133\t\n134\t### User commands\n135\t\n136\t| Command | Description |\n137\t|---------|-------------|\n138\t| `/new <name>` | Create a new forum topic and start a Claude Code session |\n139\t| `/cwd` | Show the current working directory for this session |\n140\t| `/model [name]` | View or set the active model |\n141\t| `/haiku` | Switch to Claude Haiku |\n142\t| `/sonnet` | Switch to Claude Sonnet |\n143\t| `/opus` | Switch to Claude Opus |\n144\t| `/color [name]` | Set topic icon color (`active`, `complete`, `blocked`, `error`, `review`, `research`) |\n145\t| `/notify [mode]` | Set notification mode (`live`, `summary`, `quiet`) |\n146\t| `/context <thread_id>` | Inject context from another topic into this session |\n147\t| `/snippet <name> <content>` | Save a named context snippet |\n148\t| `/snippets` | List saved snippets |\n149\t| `/info` | Show session details (model, cwd, session ID, message count, cost, notify mode, timeout) |\n150\t| `/status` | List active sessions in this group |\n151\t| `/sessions` | List all sessions across all groups |\n152\t| `/close <thread_id>` | Close a session (generates and pins a summary) |\n153\t| `/cancel [thread_id]` | Cancel the running request |\n154\t| `/dispatch [on\\|off\\|default]` | Toggle orchestrator mode |\n155\t| `/timeout [N]` | Set per-topic timeout in seconds (`0` = use group default) |\n156\t| `/cost` | Show cost breakdown (group total / daily trend / per-topic / per-user) |\n157\t| `/budget [amount]` | View or set the group budget |\n158\t| `/parallel <prompts>` | Run up to 5 prompts in parallel (separate with `---` on its own line) |\n159\t| `/bg <command>` | Run a shell command in the background and stream output to the topic |\n160\t| `/jobs` | List background jobs |\n161\t| `/kill <job_id>` | Kill a background job |\n162\t| `/ping` | Check proxy latency |\n163\t| `/version` | Show bridge and proxy versions |\n164\t| `/help` | Show help |\n165\t\n166\t### Admin-only commands\n167\t\n168\t| Command | Description |\n169\t|---------|-------------|\n170\t| `/cwd <path>` | Set group working directory (also registers the group) |\n171\t| `/permission [mode]` | Set Claude permission mode |\n172\t| `/config [setting] [value]` | View or set `permission_mode`, `allowed_tools`, or `disallowed_tools` |\n173\t| `/update [do]` | Check for or apply a self-update |\n174\t| `/adduser <id> [role]` | Add an allowed user |\n175\t| `/removeuser <id>` | Remove a user |\n176\t| `/users` | List allowed users |\n177\t\n178\tRate limiting: 30 messages per minute per user.\n179\t\n180\t---\n181\t\n182\t## Building\n183\t\n184\tRequires Go 1.25+.\n185\t\n186\t```bash\n187\t# Build all three binaries (bin/proxy, bin/bridge, bin/dashboard)\n188\tmake build\n189\t\n190\t# Build individual components\n191\tmake proxy\n192\tmake bridge\n193\tmake dashboard\n194\t\n195\t# Run tests\n196\tmake test\n197\t\n198\t# Vet\n199\tmake vet\n200\t\n201\t# Build the proxy Docker image\n202\tmake docker\n203\t```\n204\t\n205\tThe resulting Docker image is scratch-based and approximately 5 MB.\n206\t\n207\t---\n208\t\n209\t## Deployment\n210\t\n211\t### Proxy (Docker container)\n212\t\n213\tThe proxy runs as a stateless container. It only needs the bot token and a writable path for its offset-tracking database.\n214\t\n215\t```yaml\n216\t# Example docker-compose snippet\n217\tservices:\n218\t  proxy:\n219\t    image: telegram-claude-bridge:<version>\n220\t    environment:\n221\t      BOT_TOKEN: \"<your-telegram-bot-token>\"\n222\t      PROXY_LISTEN_ADDR: \":8080\"\n223\t      PROXY_DB_PATH: \"/data/proxy.db\"\n224\t    volumes:\n225\t      - ./proxy-data:/data\n226\t    ports:\n227\t      - \"8080:8080\"\n228\t```\n229\t\n230\t### Bridge (systemd service)\n231\t\n232\tThe bridge runs directly on the host so it has access to `tmux`, `claude`, and the user's Anthropic session. A sample unit file is in `deploy/telegram-claude-bridge.service`.\n233\t\n234\t```ini\n235\t[Unit]\n236\tDescription=Telegram Claude Bridge\n237\tAfter=network.target\n238\t\n239\t[Service]\n240\tType=notify\n241\tWatchdogSec=60\n242\tEnvironmentFile=/etc/telegram-claude-bridge/env\n243\tExecStart=/usr/local/bin/bridge\n244\tRestart=on-failure\n245\t\n246\t[Install]\n247\tWantedBy=multi-user.target\n248\t```\n249\t\n250\tThe bridge implements `sd_notify` (`Type=notify`) with a 60 s watchdog, so systemd will restart it automatically if it hangs.\n251\t\n252\t### Telegram bot setup\n253\t\n254\t1. Create a bot via [@BotFather](https://t.me/BotFather) and copy the token.\n255\t2. Enable **Groups** and **Group Admin** permissions for the bot.\n256\t3. In your Telegram group, enable **Topics** (Supergroup setting).\n257\t4. Add the bot to the group and promote it so it can manage topics and pin messages.\n258\t5. Set `ADMIN_USER_ID` to your Telegram user ID to bootstrap the initial admin on first start.\n259\t6. Send `/cwd <path>` from the admin account to register the group and set the working directory.\n260\t\n261\t---\n262\t\n263\t## Version\n264\t\n265\tCurrent version: **0.3.0**\n266\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"fc771a1b-2288-4e9b-989d-07dfd2527f7c","uuid":"632379d9-87b8-4183-b164-4913fecb1e04","timestamp":"2026-07-02T18:10:54.750Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/telegram-claude-bridge/README.md","content":"# telegram-claude-bridge\n\nA bridge that connects a Telegram bot to [Claude Code](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code) CLI sessions, letting users interact with Claude directly from Telegram group forum topics. Each topic gets a persistent Claude Code session running in tmux; the bridge routes messages in, streams responses back, and keeps sessions warm between turns. Uses Anthropic subscription billing (interactive PTY), not API credits.\n\n---\n\n## How it works\n\nThe system is split into two processes:\n\n**Proxy** — A lightweight container that holds the Telegram bot token, long-polls the Telegram `getUpdates` endpoint, and exposes an internal HTTP API (`/updates`, `/send`, `/edit`, etc.) for the bridge to consume. It holds no session state.\n\n**Bridge** — The stateful brain, running as a systemd service on the bare-metal host. It polls the proxy, manages Claude Code sessions, spawns `claude` inside tmux panes (one pane per forum topic), streams responses back via the proxy, and stores session metadata in a local SQLite database.\n\n**Dashboard** (optional) — A Bubbletea TUI that displays real-time session health, in-flight messages, and cost data over a Unix socket.\n\n### Claude invocation\n\nThe bridge maintains a tmux session named `telegram-bridge`. Each active Telegram forum topic maps to one tmux window. Claude Code is launched with configurable permissions:\n\n```\nclaude <permission_flags> --model <model> [--resume <session_id>]\n```\n\nPermission flags are determined by the group's `permission_mode` setting:\n- `bypassPermissions` → `--dangerously-skip-permissions`\n- `acceptEdits`, `plan`, `dontAsk` → `--permission-mode <mode>`\n\n**Note:** The current implementation always passes `--dangerously-skip-permissions` regardless of the stored `permission_mode` (tracked issue). The `permission_mode` is configurable via `/config permission_mode <mode>` or `/permission <mode>`, but does not yet affect runtime behavior.\n\nPanes stay warm between messages (45 s idle threshold). A Claude stop-hook writes the final response to a file that the bridge polls; it falls back to PTY screen-scraping if the hook is not configured. On session ID loss, up to 40 messages of history are prepended from SQLite to restore context.\n\n---\n\n## Features\n\n### Notification modes\n\nPer-topic, configurable via `/notify` or natural language:\n\n| Mode | Behavior |\n|------|----------|\n| `live` (default) | Progressively edits one Telegram message as Claude streams (1 s debounce) |\n| `summary` | Posts a placeholder, edits to the final response only when done |\n| `quiet` | No updates during processing; posts only on completion |\n\n### Natural language intent detection\n\nThe bridge intercepts common phrases before forwarding them to Claude:\n\n- **Cancel:** \"cancel\", \"stop\", \"abort\"\n- **Model switch:** \"use opus\", \"think harder\", \"fast mode\", etc.\n- **Notify mode:** \"stream updates\", \"quiet mode\", etc.\n- **Cost / status queries**\n- **Session control:** \"close session\", \"new session\"\n- **Timeout adjustments**\n\n### Media support\n\n| Type | Handling |\n|------|----------|\n| Photos | Injected as image attachments (10 MB limit) |\n| Voice messages | Transcribed via `whisper` CLI, text prepended to prompt |\n| Audio files (MP3, M4A, FLAC, WAV, OGG) | Same as voice messages |\n| Video / video notes | Keyframes extracted + audio transcribed; both fed to Claude |\n| Documents | Passed as file attachments (50 MB limit) |\n\n### Dispatcher / orchestrator mode\n\nWhen enabled (`/dispatch on`), Claude receives two synthetic tools:\n\n- `spawn_worker` — the bridge spawns a headless Claude instance and injects the result back\n- `update_progress` — posts a status message to the topic immediately\n\n### Self-update\n\nThe bridge checks a configurable remote for new versions on a background interval and applies updates with `/update do`. Update checking can be disabled by setting `UPDATE_INTERVAL_MINUTES=0`.\n\n### Session lifecycle\n\n- One session per `(chat_id, thread_id)` pair\n- Messages arriving during processing are batched in a per-topic queue (32 messages deep)\n- Sessions resume across restarts via `--resume <session_id>`\n- Stale sessions cleaned up automatically (configurable interval and TTL)\n- On close: Claude (Haiku) generates a summary and pins it to the topic\n\n---\n\n## Prerequisites\n\nThe following must be available on the host running the bridge:\n\n- **tmux** — session multiplexer that hosts Claude Code panes\n- **claude** — [Claude Code CLI](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code), logged in with an active Anthropic subscription\n- **whisper** — [whisper.cpp](https://github.com/ggerganov/whisper.cpp) or compatible CLI (required only for voice/audio transcription)\n- **git** — required only if self-update is enabled\n\nThe proxy runs as a Docker container and has no host-level dependencies beyond a container runtime.\n\n---\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Proxy environment variables\n\n| Variable | Default | Description |\n|----------|---------|-------------|\n| `BOT_TOKEN` (or `TELEGRAM_TOKEN`) | — | **Required.** Telegram bot token. |\n| `PROXY_LISTEN_ADDR` | `:8080` | HTTP listen address |\n| `PROXY_DB_PATH` | `proxy.db` | SQLite DB path for offset tracking |\n| `POLL_TIMEOUT` | `30` | Telegram long-poll timeout (seconds) |\n\n### Bridge environment variables\n\n| Variable | Default | Description |\n|----------|---------|-------------|\n| `PROXY_URL` | — | **Required.** Base URL of the proxy (e.g. `http://localhost:8080`) |\n| `BRIDGE_DB_PATH` | `bridge.db` | SQLite DB path |\n| `ALLOWED_CHAT_ID` | `0` (all) | Restrict to a single Telegram chat ID |\n| `POLL_TIMEOUT` | `30` | Long-poll timeout (seconds) |\n| `SESSION_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MINUTES` | `60` | Stale session cleanup interval (`0` = disabled) |\n| `SESSION_TTL_HOURS` | `168` (7 days) | Age at which a session is considered stale |\n| `CLOSE_INACTIVE_TOPICS` | `false` | Close Telegram topics for stale sessions |\n| `ADMIN_USER_ID` | `0` | Bootstrap initial admin on startup |\n| `ADMIN_CHAT_ID` | `0` | Chat ID for crash-loop alerts (via ExecStopPost) |\n| `EVENT_PUBLISHING_ENABLED` | `false` | Enable dashboard Unix socket events |\n| `EVENT_SOCKET_PATH` | `/tmp/telegram-bridge-events.sock` | Dashboard socket path |\n| `UPDATE_INTERVAL_MINUTES` | `5` | Self-update check interval (`0` = disabled) |\n\n---\n\n## Commands reference\n\n### User commands\n\n| Command | Description |\n|---------|-------------|\n| `/new <name>` | Create a new forum topic and start a Claude Code session |\n| `/cwd` | Show the current working directory for this session |\n| `/model [name]` | View or set the active model |\n| `/haiku` | Switch to Claude Haiku |\n| `/sonnet` | Switch to Claude Sonnet |\n| `/opus` | Switch to Claude Opus |\n| `/color [name]` | Set topic icon color (`active`, `complete`, `blocked`, `error`, `review`, `research`) |\n| `/notify [mode]` | Set notification mode (`live`, `summary`, `quiet`) |\n| `/context <thread_id>` | Inject context from another topic into this session |\n| `/snippet <name> <content>` | Save a named context snippet |\n| `/snippets` | List saved snippets |\n| `/info` | Show session details (model, cwd, session ID, message count, cost, notify mode, timeout) |\n| `/status` | List active sessions in this group |\n| `/sessions` | List all sessions across all groups |\n| `/close <thread_id>` | Close a session (generates and pins a summary) |\n| `/cancel [thread_id]` | Cancel the running request |\n| `/dispatch [on\\|off\\|default]` | Toggle orchestrator mode |\n| `/timeout [N]` | Set per-topic timeout in seconds (`0` = use group default) |\n| `/cost` | Show cost breakdown (group total / daily trend / per-topic / per-user) |\n| `/budget [amount]` | View or set the group budget |\n| `/parallel <prompts>` | Run up to 5 prompts in parallel (separate with `---` on its own line) |\n| `/bg <command>` | Run a shell command in the background and stream output to the topic |\n| `/jobs` | List background jobs |\n| `/kill <job_id>` | Kill a background job |\n| `/ping` | Check proxy latency |\n| `/version` | Show bridge and proxy versions |\n| `/help` | Show help |\n\n### Admin-only commands\n\n| Command | Description |\n|---------|-------------|\n| `/cwd <path>` | Set group working directory (also registers the group) |\n| `/permission [mode]` | Set Claude permission mode |\n| `/config [setting] [value]` | View or set `permission_mode`, `allowed_tools`, or `disallowed_tools` |\n| `/update [do]` | Check for or apply a self-update |\n| `/adduser <id> [role]` | Add an allowed user |\n| `/removeuser <id>` | Remove a user |\n| `/users` | List allowed users |\n\nRate limiting: 30 messages per minute per user.\n\n---\n\n## Building\n\nRequires Go 1.25+.\n\n```bash\n# Build all three binaries (bin/proxy, bin/bridge, bin/dashboard)\nmake build\n\n# Build individual components\nmake proxy\nmake bridge\nmake dashboard\n\n# Run tests\nmake test\n\n# Vet\nmake vet\n\n# Build the proxy Docker image\nmake docker\n```\n\nThe resulting Docker image is scratch-based and approximately 5 MB.\n\n---\n\n## Deployment\n\n### Proxy (Docker container)\n\nThe proxy runs as a stateless container. It only needs the bot token and a writable path for its offset-tracking database.\n\n```yaml\n# Example docker-compose snippet\nservices:\n  proxy:\n    image: telegram-claude-bridge:<version>\n    environment:\n      BOT_TOKEN: \"<your-telegram-bot-token>\"\n      PROXY_LISTEN_ADDR: \":8080\"\n      PROXY_DB_PATH: \"/data/proxy.db\"\n    volumes:\n      - ./proxy-data:/data\n    ports:\n      - \"8080:8080\"\n```\n\n### Bridge (systemd service)\n\nThe bridge runs directly on the host so it has access to `tmux`, `claude`, and the user's Anthropic session. A sample unit file is in `deploy/telegram-claude-bridge.service`.\n\n```ini\n[Unit]\nDescription=Telegram Claude Bridge\nAfter=network.target\n\n[Service]\nType=notify\nWatchdogSec=60\nEnvironmentFile=/etc/telegram-claude-bridge/env\nExecStart=/usr/local/bin/bridge\nRestart=on-failure\n\n[Install]\nWantedBy=multi-user.target\n```\n\nThe bridge implements `sd_notify` (`Type=notify`) with a 60 s watchdog, so systemd will restart it automatically if it hangs.\n\n### Telegram bot setup\n\n1. Create a bot via [@BotFather](https://t.me/BotFather) and copy the token.\n2. Enable **Groups** and **Group Admin** permissions for the bot.\n3. In your Telegram group, enable **Topics** (Supergroup setting).\n4. Add the bot to the group and promote it so it can manage topics and pin messages.\n5.
